selected_goal: Prevent rendered question prompt images from retaining a later standalone top-level question when the later anchor was missed by the main span detector.
why_this_now: The user called out 2008-2021 papers where one question image includes multiple questions. Same-page trailing contamination is one of the highest-impact crop errors because the question prompt image can show the next question even when question spans are otherwise mapped.
repo_evidence: src/exam_bank/image_rendering.py trims furniture and neighboring content, but the existing check relied on known neighboring question starts.
Some historical PDF layouts expose the next question as a standalone anchor candidate that is not present in the parsed question start list.
tests/test_image_rendering.py already has synthetic crop-region tests that can isolate crop trimming behavior without needing a full PDF run.
acceptance_criteria: A prompt crop region is removed or shortened when it encounters a later top-level foreign question anchor on the same page.
The fix uses existing anchor detection machinery rather than broad text-string heuristics.
Existing image-rendering tests continue to pass.
test_expectations: Add a focused failing test where question 5's crop includes a missed question 6 anchor.
Run the new test before implementation and confirm it fails.
Run the focused test and the full image-rendering test file after implementation.
implementation_boundaries: Do not change generated output files.
Do not alter question-number parsing outside the crop-rendering boundary decision.
Keep the change local to prompt-region trimming.
repo_hygiene_risk: Low: bounded source and test edits only; no canonical outputs or generated datasets are rewritten.
rollback_plan: Remove the new crop-boundary helper and the synthetic test, returning prompt trimming to the previous neighbor-start-only behavior.
done_when: The focused foreign-question prompt crop test passes.
The full image-rendering test module passes.
No generated output files are modified.
